reason: While God and the leaders (God, Moses, Aaron, and Pharaoh) clearly make major decisions throughout the text, the passage also depicts other individuals making decisions. Notably, Pharaoh's servants express their own judgment and opinions (e.g., 'עַד־ מָתַי יִהְיֶה זֶה לָנוּ לְמוֹקֵשׁ' - 'How long will this be a snare to us?'), influencing events. Some Egyptian servants make decisions to protect their livestock based on fearing God's word ('הַיָּרֵא אֶת־ דְּבַר יְהוָה'). Additionally, Egyptian magicians actively attempt to replicate miracles, demonstrating agency. The text also shows that ordinary Egyptians and Israelites engage in responsive decision-making regarding the plagues, indicating that decision-making power extends beyond just leaders and God.
